text: In these dimly lit hallways and creative spaces, I feel both curiosity and solitude. The emptiness of the first scene evokes a sense of anticipation, as if waiting for something to break the silence. Entering the second space, surrounded by the language of innovation and research, my mind shifts to wonder and reflection. There is excitement in the air, a hint that ideas are forging here, yet the absence of people gives it all a dreamlike, contemplative quality. I am both observer and participant, caught between the hushed expectancy of what might come and the latent energy of human creativity.
